description: Fig. 6 presents a quantitative line plot illustrating the strain-dependent evolution of deformation substructure dimensions in the 8Al steel (Fe-35Mn-8Al-0.1C) during tensile deformation. The graph displays three statistical curves with error bars against engineering strain (0–28%): the black curve (α_SB) tracks the average slip band spacing in ferrite, refining from approximately 780 nm at 3% strain to about 80 nm at 28% strain; the red curve (γ_SB+DCB) shows the combined slip band and dislocation cell block size in austenite, decreasing from roughly 900 nm to 240 nm; and the blue curve (γ_DC) represents the dislocation cell size in austenite, measured at higher strains (20–28%) and reducing from approximately 280 nm to 220 nm. These data quantitatively demonstrate the dynamic refinement of dislocation patterns in the duplex microstructure, where ferrite undergoes continuous slip band refinement while austenite transitions from planar slip to cellular structures. The measurements provide critical microstructural parameters (D values) for the analytical strengthening model and support the dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR) mechanism underlying the steel's enhanced strain hardening capacity.
